title: The Duke's Laws codify English rule

primary.name: Historical Society of the New York Courts — The Hempstead Convention (Duke's Laws, 1665)

title: New York's first assembly passes a Charter of Liberties

primary.name: Historical Society of the New York Courts — Colonial New York under British Rule (1683 Charter of Liberties and Privileges)

title: The Dongan Charter incorporates New York City

primary.name: NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission — Dongan Charter (1686)

title: Trinity Church receives its royal charter

primary.name: Trinity Church — parish chartered 1697 by Gov. Benjamin Fletcher on behalf of King William III

title: Captain Kidd is hanged for piracy

primary.name: Library of Congress — The Arraignment, Tryal, and Condemnation of Captain William Kidd (1701 trial record; guilty of murder and piracy, executed at Execution Dock May 23)
Gilder Lehrman — Gov. Robert Hunter's report to the Lords of Trade, June 23, 1712 (the revolt occurred the 'sixth day of April')

title: The Montgomerie Charter reorganizes city government

primary.name: NYPL — Montgomerie Charter, sealed by Gov. John Montgomerie January 15, 1730 (original parchment)

title: The Mill Street Synagogue, first in North America

primary.name: Congregation Shearith Israel — Mill Street Synagogue consecrated April 8, 1730, first purpose-built synagogue in continental North America

title: The Zenger trial and freedom of the press

primary.name: Historical Society of the New York Courts — Crown v. Zenger (not-guilty verdict, August 4, 1735)
Historical Society of the New York Courts — New York Slave Conspiracy Trials of 1741 (Justice Daniel Horsmanden; ~30 executed)

title: The Stamp Act Congress meets in New York

primary.name: Avalon Project (Yale Law) — Declaration of Rights and Grievances of the Stamp Act Congress, October 19, 1765
Fraunces Tavern Museum — The New York Tea Party (April 22, 1774; ship London; the Nancy turned back)

title: The Declaration is read to Washington's troops

primary.name: Founders Online — Washington's General Orders, 9 July 1776 (brigades drawn up at 6 o'clock, Declaration 'to be read with an audible voice')
Trinity Church — 1776, Trinity Church and the American Revolution (fire September 21, 1776; Trinity destroyed, St. Paul's Chapel saved)
Fraunces Tavern Museum — Washington's Farewell in the Long Room, December 4, 1783
